title: Two agent skills: teach code changes instead of dumping diffs
glance: Adds the first building blocks: explain-diff and explain-micro-world agent skills — playbooks that tell Cursor agents how to write teaching documents about code.
backgroundDeep: A git diff shows line changes but rarely explains why or how pieces connect. An agent skill is a markdown file in .cursor/skills/ that agents read when relevant — like a playbook.
backgroundNarrow: The repo had incremental commit rules but no process for teaching humans what agent code does.
goal: Give the agent recipes for literate diffs (essays + quiz) and micro-worlds (playgrounds you choose before the agent builds).
intuition: This commit is instructions only — templates and scripts land in commit #3.

steps.label: explain-diff skill
steps.body: Defines plain-language teaching, Background → Intuition → Code → Quiz, and output paths under docs/understanding/.

steps.label: explain-micro-world skill
steps.body: Propose 2–3 playground options → human picks → build smallest useful toy. Inspired by Papert's Mathland.

options: A markdown instruction file agents read
Prose explaining changes in narrative order
